hero.headline: Everybody has a home, and everybody eats.
hero.subheadline: Byron Sigcho Lopez is a husband, father of little triplets, math teacher & economist, naturalized immigrant, and two-term elected Democratic Socialist Alderman from Chicago's Pilsen neighborhood.
hero.context: The Democratic Primary Election in IL-04's Congressional District was rigged! On March 17th, many of our neighbors across Illinois had 5+ choices on their Democratic Primary ballot. Voters in our district only had ONE because the political establishment chose on our behalf. Byron needs over 10,816 valid petition signatures in order to appear on your ballot on November 3rd. We need your help to overcome a system rigged against unbought candidates!

bio.label: Meet the Organizer
bio.headline: He wasn't born here, but he followed his heart home as quickly as he could.
bio.p1: Byron is an immigrant who came to the United States alone when he was 17 years old. His life is a testament to what happens when strangers become neighbors and family.
bio.p2: Byron Sigcho Lopez is the only elected official in this race who has delivered material benefits for the working people of Chicago, with a record of coalition building with every neighbor willing to come together to solve the problems we face, together. He has courageously fought the corruption of the Illinois political machine, helping take down corrupt charter school networks looting from our children's future, and even taking campaign finance corruption all the way to the Illinois Supreme Court.
bio.p3: Byron earned his Bachelor's in Mathematics and Business Administration from Cumberland University and his Master's in Economics from the University of Illinois at Chicago.
bio.p4: Byron lives in the Pilsen neighborhood with his wife, Loreen, their 3-year-old triplets, a dog, and two cats.

platform.label: The Platform
platform.headline: Where everybody has a home, and everybody eats.

housing.title: Housing as a Human Right
housing.desc: Our country can and must provide dignified and safe housing for every veteran and neighbor who needs a helping hand. National tenant bill of rights and expand social housing developments to lower the rent and lower the cost of buying a home.

economic.title: An Economy Putting People and Planet First
economic.desc: Tax the billionaires and invest our resources in creating sustainable union jobs for every worker in our communities. Raise the minimum wage to $30/hour. No bailouts for banks or tech bubbles. Investments in supporting local entrepreneurs across IL-04. Civilian climate corps jobs for green energy, natural spaces, and community resilience.

education.title: Literacy, Curiosity, Creativity, and Inspiration Now
education.desc: Every student, 0 to 100, deserves the opportunity to be inspired, pursue their curiosity, nurture their creativity, and expand their literacy. Fight to restore the U.S. Department of Education. Invest in fully funded public education and provide every student and family with robust wraparound services and extracurricular activities.

healthcare.title: Healthcare for All
healthcare.desc: Our resources are not endless and not one more dollar should go to greedy middlemen who do not improve the health of our people. We must pass Medicare for All, and exponentially grow our investment in the National Institutes of Health (NIH) to put more of our most curious people spending their time eliminating and curing premature causes of death or chronic illnesses like heart disease, cancer, and mental illness.

immigration.title: Abolish ICE, Create a Pathway to Citizenship for Our Neighbors
immigration.desc: No human being is illegal. Abolish ICE and end the militarized deportation machine terrorizing our communities. Ban ICE from operating on public property, shut down detention centers, and end the surveillance state targeting immigrant families. Create a clear, humane pathway to citizenship for the millions of neighbors who build our communities every day.

foreignpolicy.title: Foreign Policy
foreignpolicy.desc: No taxpayer dollars for apartheid and genocide. End all military aid and weapons shipments sustaining the occupation of Palestine and the genocide of its people. End the illegal, unauthorized war with Iran. Prohibit investment in Israel Bonds and contracts with corporations manufacturing weapons used for ethnic cleansing. Demand an end to U.S. imperialism and redirect those billions toward our communities at home.

anointment.label: Why Independent?
anointment.headline: The Anointment
anointment.text: When Rep. Jesús "Chuy" García announced his retirement, he didn't give voters a choice. He filed for a fifth term, then moments before the filing deadline, his former chief of staff filed nominating petitions collected in just days. García withdrew and endorsed her, handing the seat to an uncontested successor. She needed only 697 signatures. Independent candidates must collect over 10,816. This is not democracy. This is an anointment.

footer.ticker_text: Average donation: $27. No corporate PAC money. Ever.
footer.paid_for: PAID FOR BY BYRON SIGCHO LOPEZ FOR CONGRESS
footer.copyright: © 2026 Byron Sigcho Lopez for Congress. All rights reserved.

meet.hero_sub: From Ecuador to Tennessee to Pilsen. Byron Sigcho-Lopez came to this country alone at 17 and has spent his life fighting alongside the working class as a teacher, researcher, organizer, and unapologetic democratic socialist.
meet.origin_p1: Byron first came to the United States alone as a teenager, settling in Tennessee and then moving to the Chicago area and Pilsen over 10 years ago. He was born and raised in Quito, Ecuador and came to the US following political instability in the country, and a tragic car accident that left his mother in a coma.
meet.origin_p2: Emergency services were nearly non-existent during this period, and his family's lives were saved by passing motorists who stopped, rescued his family from the wreckage, and drove them to the hospital. When his mother awoke from the coma, she insisted her eldest leave the country for his last year of high school.
meet.origin_p3: When Byron arrived in Tennessee at the age of 17, he knew no one in the country and did not speak English. He was taken in by a generous family who gave him a home and helped him pursue an education.
meet.education_p1: Byron first visited Chicago with his Tennessee family who had loved ones in the Chicago area. During this first trip, they brought Byron to Pilsen to have dinner at the Nuevo Leon restaurant.
meet.education_p2: After graduating high school in Tennessee, Byron was able to attend college on a soccer scholarship after receiving an honorable mention for the All-State Tennessee Soccer Team in high school. Byron graduated from Cumberland University with his Bachelors in Mathematics and Business Administration.
meet.education_p3: He was also awarded the prestigious President's Award for his exceptional academic performance, and many contributions and successes in the school community during his time there. After graduation, Byron returned to Chicago and eventually settled in Pilsen to start his life.
meet.record_desc: Byron became an adult education teacher at the University of Illinois at Chicago and founded the UIC Center for Literacy's Spanish literacy program, making the GED program at UIC much more widely accessible to Spanish speaking immigrants and residents. Seeing the need for wrap-around services, Byron also brought a case worker into the program to ensure his students' families received resources and needed services.
meet.record_desc2: In addition to Byron's work as a teacher, he also volunteered as a youth soccer coach at Pilsen Academy. Byron earned a Master's Degree in Economics from UIC while also working as a lead researcher. He began collaborating alongside the Chicago Teachers Union in investigating the financial relationships between charter school networks like UNO, school funding, and Chicago's political elite, and organized with parents and teachers like Karen Lewis to fight for fully funded public schools.
meet.schools_desc: After Rahm Emanuel announced that he would be closing a hundred thirty Chicago Public Schools, Byron became involved in the Pilsen Alliance where he organized with the community to push to keep the schools open.
meet.first_run_desc: In 2015, Byron was encouraged by community leaders Nellie Cotton and Kit Duffy to run for Alderman against the machine - now disgraced former Alderman Danny Solis. Byron came in second in the 5-way race.
meet.alliance_desc: Byron eventually served as the Director of Pilsen Alliance where he became a leader and an activist in the fight against gentrification, displacement, and corruption.
meet.dsa_desc: He joined forces with organizers from the Chicago Democratic Socialists of America as he was partnering on the coalition to Lift the Ban on Rent Control across the state of Illinois, and became a member then and there.
meet.elected_headline: A Grassroots Movement, Not a Machine Candidacy
meet.elected_p1: In 2019, Byron ran a grassroots campaign for Alderman of the 25th Ward. He committed to refuse political contributions from developers and big corporations - a commitment he has upheld to this day and beyond. Since taking office, any resident now can personally meet with Byron at his weekly Ward Night, and all residents can receive compassion, support, and city services from the devoted public servants who staff the 25th Ward office.
meet.elected_p2: In 2023, big money and the political establishment lined up against Byron. Byron took democratic establishment and corrupt Danny Solis all the way to the Illinois Supreme Court over his unethical campaign finance practices. The voters of the 25th Ward sent him back to City Hall.
meet.elected_p3: As an elected official, he used the power of his office to put the people's power into the institutions that needed it - under his leadership, change is happening.
meet.achieve_housing_desc: Byron has preserved and created over 800 units of affordable housing across Chicago and as Chairman of the Committee on Housing and Real Estate, made Chicago the largest city to pass a $135 million dollar investment via the Green Social Housing Initiative, to build sustainable housing and help address the housing crisis in Illinois.
meet.achieve_wardnight_desc: Every community member has the opportunity to speak directly with their elected official every Tuesday at the Ward Office - the accountability is a commitment.
meet.achieve_health_desc: The Pilsen Food Pantry has grown to serve more than ever. Perpetrators of heinous crimes are held accountable more often and public safety has become more possible for the community to organize for. A dental clinic accessible to the people was opened and a mental health clinic as well.
meet.achieve_fight_desc: Property tax payers know that their elected official has voted against every single proposed property tax increase, and worked to fight for a statewide millionaire surcharge and wealth taxes on the billionaires. When Trump sent ICE to our communities, Byron faced them head on with the full weight of his office and his community ensuring that Bovino and other thugs were held accountable to the furthest extent possible in court.
meet.elected_call: Byron has changed what was possible in public service on the municipal level and he isn't stopping. We need him in Congress.
meet.family_desc: Byron, and his wife Loreen, live in Pilsen with their little triplets (who attend public school), and their dog, Buster, and their two cats Dos and 三歲.
meet.caption_ecuador: Byron with his family in Quito, Ecuador
meet.caption_cumberland: Receiving the President's Award at Cumberland University
meet.caption_schools: Fighting for investment in our neighborhood schools
meet.cta_headline: Send an Unbought Organizer to Congress.

issues.hero_headline: A Platform for Working People, Not the Billionaire Class.
issues.hero_sub: Byron is fighting to guarantee a baseline standard of dignity for working families, seniors, teachers, and marginalized groups across IL-04.
issues.grid_headline: Where everybody has a home, and everybody eats.
issues.housing_desc: Our country can and must provide dignified and safe housing for every veteran and neighbor who needs a helping hand. National tenant bill of rights and expand social housing developments to lower the rent and lower the cost of buying a home.
issues.economic_title: An Economy Putting People and Planet First
issues.economic_desc: Tax the billionaires and invest our resources in creating jobs that bring sustainable union jobs for every worker in our communities. Raise the minimum wage to $30/hour. No bailouts for banks or tech bubbles. Investments in supporting local entrepreneurs that form the backbone of our economy across IL-04. Civilian climate corps jobs to invest in green energy projects, expansion of natural and recreational spaces, and resilience around storms, floods, heatwaves, and disasters in our communities.
issues.education_title: Literacy, Curiosity, Creativity, and Inspiration Now
issues.education_desc: Every student, 0 to 100, deserves the opportunity to be inspired, pursue their curiosity, nurture their creativity, and expand their literacy. Fight to restore the U.S. Department of Education and the civil rights benefits for all learners. Invest in fully funded public education based on student need and provide every student and family with robust wraparound services and extracurricular activities.
issues.healthcare_desc: Our resources are not endless and not one more dollar should go to greedy middlemen who do not improve the health of our people. We must pass Medicare for All, and exponentially grow our investment in the National Institutes of Health (NIH) to put more of our most curious people spending their time eliminating and curing premature causes of death or chronic illnesses like heart disease, cancer, and mental illness.
issues.immigration_title: Abolish ICE, Create a Pathway to Citizenship for Our Neighbors
issues.immigration_desc: No human being is illegal. Abolish ICE and end the militarized deportation machine terrorizing our communities. Ban ICE from operating on public property, shut down detention centers like the Wyatt Detention Center, and end the sale of surveillance data from Flock, Ring, and other systems to law enforcement. Create a clear, humane pathway to citizenship for the millions of neighbors who build our communities every day. Enforce accountability for police officers and demilitarize law enforcement.
issues.foreignpolicy_desc: No taxpayer dollars for apartheid and genocide. End all military aid and weapons shipments sustaining the occupation of Palestine and the genocide of its people. End the illegal, unauthorized war with Iran. Prohibit state investment in Israel Bonds and contracting with any corporation that manufactures or ships weapons or technology sustaining genocide, apartheid, ethnic cleansing, and the occupation of Palestine. Demand an end to U.S. imperialism and redirect those billions toward our communities at home.
issues.unbought_text: Byron completely rejects corporate PAC money and contributions from large real estate developers. He only answers to the working-class coalition of the 4th District. His campaign is funded by small-dollar donations from neighbors, teachers, union members, and working families. This is what it means to be truly independent.
issues.cta_headline: Fight for This Platform. Sign the Petition.

machine.hero_sub: We are building an independent, working-class movement to defeat a corrupt political machine that believes it can govern without the consent of the people.
machine.movement_intro: Byron Sigcho-Lopez is an ideologically committed democratic socialist and a firebrand who has spent his entire career fighting for the multiracial working class. He is not a politician looking for a promotion. He is an organizer who was called to serve by his community.
machine.unbought_text: Unlike establishment politicians, Byron completely rejects corporate PAC money and contributions from real estate developers. His campaign is powered entirely by thousands of small-dollar grassroots donations from teachers, nurses, union members, and working families. He has upheld this commitment since his first campaign in 2015 and has never wavered.
machine.tested_text: Byron's unique power comes from bottom-up organizing. As 25th Ward Alderperson, he passed anti-displacement legislation to protect homeowners from predatory developers, created over 800 units of affordable housing, stood uncompromisingly with the Chicago Teachers Union, doubled the Affordable Requirements Ordinance minimum, and joined a hunger strike to fight environmental racism. These are not campaign promises. These are receipts.
machine.pullquote: "I have never taken a dollar from a corporation or a developer. I never will. My loyalty is to the people of this district, not to the machine."
machine.local_p1: When Rep. Chuy Garcia decided to retire from Congress, he did not announce it publicly and let the voters decide. Instead, he waited until the final hours before the filing deadline. This ensured that only one person, his former Chief of Staff Patty Garcia, could get on the ballot with just 697 signatures. The seat was handed over in a backroom, not won in an election.
machine.local_p2: This maneuver earned a rare reprimand from the U.S. House Ethics Committee for "election subversion." While the machine's candidate relies on institutional inertia, frontline PACs, and a rigged filing system, Byron is building a grassroots base one doorstep at a time, funded entirely by working people.
machine.thesis: "Democracy cannot be preserved nationally if it is subverted locally."
machine.timeline_intro: What happened in IL-04 is not an isolated incident. It is part of a broader, dangerous pattern of the establishment insulating itself from the working class.
machine.timeline_2016: The DNC weaponized the undemocratic superdelegate system to block progressive momentum and override the will of grassroots voters.
machine.timeline_2020: The party establishment swiftly coalesced in a coordinated effort to block Bernie Sanders' grassroots movement, consolidating behind a single candidate overnight.
machine.timeline_2024: The party suppressed primary challenges and skipped debates, culminating in a late Biden drop-out and an uncompetitive process that left voters demoralized and demobilized.
machine.timeline_now: In IL-04, the same playbook is being used at the local level, handing a Congressional seat to a handpicked successor without a competitive election.
machine.fight_p1: The machine is not just trying to outspend Byron. It is actively trying to keep him off the ballot entirely. Illinois election law is designed to punish independent candidates. While the machine's anointed candidate needed only 697 signatures to get on the ballot, Byron must collect exactly 10,816 valid signatures.
machine.fight_p2: But it gets worse. The establishment will use bad-faith legal challenges to try and strike his petition signatures. Trained operatives will comb through every sheet, challenging every misspelled name, every address that is one digit off. To survive these challenges, the campaign must gather an overwhelming 40,000 to 50,000 signatures.
machine.stat_needed: Signatures actually needed to survive legal challenges
machine.fight_framing: Signing this petition is not just a legal formality. It is an act of defiance against a system designed to suppress independent voices. Every signature is a direct challenge to the machine's grip on power.
machine.cta_headline: Defy the Machine. Join the Movement.

volunteer.hero_headline: We Are The Machine's Worst Nightmare.
volunteer.hero_sub: We don't have corporate PAC money or backroom bosses. We have you. Join the multiracial working-class coalition to take back IL-04.
volunteer.blitz_p1: Illinois election law intentionally punishes independent candidates to protect the two-party duopoly. The campaign is legally mandated to collect exactly 10,816 valid signatures just to appear on the ballot, while his machine-backed opponent bypassed this requirement entirely with just 697.
volunteer.blitz_p2: The political establishment will inevitably deploy armies of lawyers and bad-faith challenges to strike valid signatures. The campaign urgently needs volunteers to help gather a massive surplus of 40,000 to 50,000 signatures to survive these attacks.
volunteer.canvass_desc: Knock on doors in Pilsen, Little Village, and the inner-ring suburbs to have meaningful, one-on-one conversations about housing, economic justice, and the fight against the machine. This is how movements are built: at the doorstep.
volunteer.ticket_desc: As an independent, Byron will not benefit from the straight-ticket Democratic vote. We need educators to teach voters how to split their ticket in November to defy the machine and cast a vote for a real representative.
volunteer.digital_desc: Reach voters where they are through rapid-response text campaigns, phone calls, and social media. This work can be done from anywhere, anytime, and is critical for expanding the movement beyond what door-knocking alone can achieve.
volunteer.signup_desc: Fill out the form and our team will reach out with next steps. Whether you have one hour or one hundred, there is a role for you in this movement.
volunteer.help_legend: How would you like to help?
volunteer.check_bilingual: Bilingual Outreach (I speak Spanish / Yo hablo español)
volunteer.compliance: By submitting, you agree to receive periodic campaign updates via SMS. Message and data rates may apply. Text STOP to unsubscribe.
volunteer.cta_headline: Can't Volunteer Today? Fund the Fight.
volunteer.cta_text: Every dollar helps buy clipboards, literature, and digital tools for the volunteer army. This campaign is entirely unbought and funded by working people like you.

petition_modal.title: Help Byron Get on the Ballot
petition_modal.subtitle: Byron needs over 10,816 valid petition signatures. Sign up below to help make sure voters have a real choice on November 3rd.
